questions.question: A shipping company has $12$ and $15$ minute delivery intervals. What is the $GCF(12, 15)$?
A traffic light has $8$ and $12$ second intervals. What is the $LCM(8, 12)$?
A truck travels $18$ miles in $3$ hours and another truck travels $24$ miles in $4$ hours. What is the $GCF(18, 24)$?
A train has $20$ and $30$ minute stops. What is the $LCM(20, 30)$?
A delivery service has packages of $9$ and $12$ items. What is the $GCF(9, 12)$?
A bus travels $15$ miles in $3$ hours and another bus travels $25$ miles in $5$ hours. What is the $LCM(15, 25)$?
A taxi company has $8$ and $10$ minute pickup intervals. What is the $GCF(8, 10)$?
A shipping crate has $6$ and $9$ foot dimensions. What is the $LCM(6, 9)$?
A delivery company has two trucks with $12$ and $15$ minute delivery intervals. How many minutes will it take for both trucks to arrive at the same time? Show your work and explain your reasoning.
A train station has two trains with $8$ and $12$ minute arrival intervals. What is the $LCM(8, 12)$ and how does it relate to the train schedule? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

teacher_rules: Emphasize the importance of using prime factorization to find GCF and LCM.
Encourage students to use real-world examples to illustrate their understanding of GCF and LCM.
Remind students to show their work and explain their reasoning in the free response questions.
